Since email is one of the most effective ways for us to directly communicate with you, we will be reaching out to our supporters regarding this deadline quite often over the course of the next 9 days!
So, we'll offer you a deal: if you click below and donate $25 or more to our campaign today, we won't send you any more fundraising emails for the rest of the month (don't worry! you'll still get other important news and updates).

name: Beth Hansen, Campaign Manager
subject: See this?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kasich For America, www
body:
Peter, I wanted to make sure that you saw the email message below from John. We have a critical fundraising deadline at the end of the month and want to show the strength of our campaign. Hope you can help us out!

-Beth

\---
From: John Weaver, Chief Strategist
Sent: September 21, 2015
To: Peter
Subject: #Momentum
[](www.JohnKasich.com)

Peter, our country needs a common-sense conservative in the White House. Gov. John Kasich is the only Midwestern governor in the race who can bring our values to Washington, D.C.

Gov. Kasich is the _only candidate_ that has the record to defeat Hillary.

**[Will you chip in $25, $50 or more right now to help us keep up our growing momentum?](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549770:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)**

[](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549771:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)

**Need proof that we're on the rise? Here are some recent highlights: **

• Gov. Kasich is running at 3rd in New Hampshire and a recent poll showed he's the only candidate defeating Hillary in New Hampshire and Ohio.

• We've received some major endorsements and keep adding more each week. [Read more here](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549772:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r).

• Gov. Kasich placed 3rd in the prestigious Michigan straw poll this past weekend - ahead of Jeb Bush and Marco Rubio.

• A recent Iowa poll had Gov. Kasich virtually tied with both Jeb Bush and Marco Rubio and we just expanded our Iowa team.

• Hillary Clinton's campaign continues to attack Gov. Kasich, demonstrating what several of her strategists have said; they're most afraid of Gov. John Kasich.

**Peter, Gov. Kasich's campaign is on the rise and with your help, we can win**. Please chip in today to help!

[

](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549771:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)
Thank you for all that you're doing to help. Let's win!

**John Weaver
**Chief Strategist

P.S. In case you missed our highlight video of Gov. Kasich at the recent debate at the Ronald Reagan Library, [watch it here:](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549773:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)

[](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549774:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)
[](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549775:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)
[](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549776:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)
[
](http://email.sermodigital.com/ct/30549777:WDO-ZamNA:m:1:958431888:1316A3CEA4A184961D943F938F5E86C0:r)
Paid for by Kasich For America, www.JohnKasich.com
This message was intended for: [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)
You were added to the system July 18, 2015.

name: Team Katie
subject: Toomey’s latest flip-flop
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate |
body:
Friend --

Pat Toomey is once again putting extreme partisan politics ahead of Pennsylvania families. This time, he walked away from his support for expanded background checks for firearm purchases.

Why? So he could avoid having a protest at a campaign event. That’s right – Toomey has backed away from commonsense gun safety measures to protect his re-election campaign.

That’s not the leadership Pennsylvania deserves. Add your name today and tell Pat Toomey to put safety first and support expanded background checks for firearm sales.

https://act.myngp.com/Forms/-1650005213966237696

Every day in America, an average of 89 people die as a result of gun violence. In 2012 alone, there were 1,453 gun deaths in Pennsylvania. Pennsylvanians have a right to be protected from gun violence, and they have the right to expect their elected officials to do what it takes to keep them safe. By bowing to extreme partisans, Senator Toomey is putting us all at risk.

A staggering 95% of Pennsylvanians agree there should be universal background checks for firearm purchases. But Pat Toomey is watching out for his well-funded special interests allies. After all, in 2010, the National Rifle Association spent more than $1.3 million to ensure a Toomey victory and maintain the status quo.

Stand up today in support of expanded background checks. Tell Pat Toomey to put Pennsylvania first.

https://act.myngp.com/Forms/-1650005213966237696

Katie is working hard to hold Toomey accountable for his failure to lead for Pennsylvania families. Together, we’ll fight to pass important legislation like universal background checks for firearm purchases. The only way we can make meaningful changes in Washington is to stand together and demand action right now.

Thanks for standing with us,

Team Katie

----------------------------------------------
| Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate |
----------------------------------------------

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please https://act.myngp.com/el/-7901929385786081280/-4618140151682433024.

Katie McGinty for Senate
PO Box 22447
Philadelphia PA 19110 United States

| Dear Friend,

I wanted to share a couple new items with you today. As you have likely seen, Gov. Scott Walker suspended his campaign for President yesterday, and [we were proud to welcome several former supporters and leaders of his campaign to Team Cruz](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=oxRiKmNZf-2B-2FjHRySYBDLy09v5edMMZm9mVbjZI6SnjywjP6VUh0OLaPqlEmU7RXst0kKI-2FPvVqHxv65dvAF9b-2BxoMQQmMG2IwB-2F7cUzoFAH69zuIhSBRrZzGCiUsLsFjit6y8MLMpcooN0nU6zh-2FJV8K0rcTC3kP-2FbI0CkYADo0-3D_enNSH--2FKzdVsl4JnefYEJ7wdQuCJhXy6BMoXbfv3ilpKodMaaPLuU5j2tdPXRtK23ZUOEBeX-2F7wqlaitGKDLheLeQC-2BeguBgG2aW-2FCjIn2fmUZx-2FOJdTY-3D). We're honored to have the support of Phyllis Gannon, Barb Pape, and Dean Hamilton of Iowa, Louie Hunter of Georgia, Assemblywoman Vicki Dooling and Assemblyman Ira and Alexis Hansen of Nevada, and many others!

In addition, last week our campaign announced that we've [surpassed 500 members](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=oxRiKmNZf-2B-2FjHRySYBDLy09v5edMMZm9mVbjZI6SnjywjP6VUh0OLaPqlEmU7RXst0kKI-2FPvVqHxv65dvAF9b7MnSGQY7wsEvmGYK5hSUKEUCId2JHQYM6PoAfkvkNpPHmHD2KFBc1652X4ZRnNtyQ-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCE1EYyLLELQsnppRHHkfOyTYjETsa294ePKLM3jBe5UG3SJ6IFyX5hMiP0IVce-2BDT8xMcYfnxZhFDgRQVn-2Bf-2BqiXY5P3gp-2FxCIYvpFo5IHvmBuhjFqmTjN1Vagf8WgIzScETMEIoaQ0zGWY5UEyRW-2FHTBt1eEtfAIyOECSbmCF5FjRkTJX2rs1bvI1xx74rwKuO8LMp-2B-2BOoAqVezzA5HwnI8J4yZnlKaeCu0IIWaPDrNZnTb8Zr7tQGINZTt167QWW7LCm197cLr3DXY7JqPHQEtguhyDEOEgvtGOW3Z9KhLebIWvnHWN4p9GVQPDvqOrRQioAw1QQRb58FaNclfL2bTSL2RKXwwVQp8xcrZhdpsS9HN6G-2Bz8nf-2F8YrDXZfeAk-3D) among our State Leadership Teams across the nation.

The rapid growth of our State Leadership Teams across America and the tremendous fundraising success we're seeing are making it increasingly clear to many that _Ted Cruz is a front runner for the Republican nomination for President_ \-- [**as evidenced by this article written by RedState 's Erick Erickson yesterday.**](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=TEeb5-2FMuQyoelyZpsAGcFKzReVUrlII77nCZF6AzGpgSzeuxGjr8xNz5ZrJEdwTMocw0xEzOAJHucIoGqskrn8Kc-2Bjzy9ot70u5sUJ1y8-2FSpbceq5cZwWHwQYS0PnFzx_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCE1EYyLLELQsnppRHHkfOyT04uCwD4e8E710S5HwT7NWx0IAmjtE-2FTM5N69EI9IDzgnheu5i09Cvdpav0XAClFTeYJ8T73cxCQwgqLyvByHrvEmA8SCzf0y4TvBIaEPwiBR7nyVBDP-2FoP-2B5niArq8hJ529nC4XgdUFUoF255FYudtr-2F4HFSnOaMDBQFzz3xmgjjvPPDKFii2cwvMOXTnKX0aXAHHYARugigMPKBQ9RjSPYRY9UeDvR5R2wCMGhjbZtKTLbEs0EnbtRtrdIUKAFALh9F3mjgOITv2NhcddiQbE52aFQYF1TuV4vpqdI-2BXSDmgMNoUr9FmJKcQrUZu2tfiW5AGzmg-2BHty4RAItrV4R8Rwa78zlnA4CBjo309goIs-3D)

So I just wanted to say thank you. Thank you for your support, thank you for making this all possible, and thank you in advance for everything that's yet to be done to ensure Ted is the Republican nominee for President!

Mark Campbell
National Political Director
Cruz for President

P.S. Tonight Ted will appear on the Kelly File on Fox News -- don't forget to tune in at 9 pm ET!

[**Ted Cruz vs. Marco Rubio: This Is Where We Are Headed**](http://em.tedcruz.org/wf/click?upn=TEeb5-2FMuQyoelyZpsAGcFKzReVUrlII77nCZF6AzGpgSzeuxGjr8xNz5ZrJEdwTMocw0xEzOAJHucIoGqskrn8Kc-2Bjzy9ot70u5sUJ1y8-2FSpbceq5cZwWHwQYS0PnFzx_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCE1EYyLLELQsnppRHHkfOyTcDCaYIINMNFZ9LhYPG0EhBfiObBebfG2HdPgRfj8hW6QPHRfjTMeKuLkkPuiiAMHEC3a2t2q8giMNEiGjmRldMkKIhtwOwYUsdifnoY6hPOxWdBh2-2FQQ4s7zt3PdisXg-2FvJW-2FPzzU2hnFegGFVczsRK-2BNd3BY58Pz1SuWMFkt2iaMMb-2FR0VfTkdJXA4UPi3-2FVejvjUv89gSc6AyNrEbO9NkJqlzAWCKjx-2B-2FvYtebs8Qg33kd4LEtvowvONcMevILFYZRcHXyGHz7mmZQOXSi7rVreT6b0-2BagyH1LJr93O-2FMIibjqPiaAPfb3D3AdwagSyhO04ReghIHlBI-2BRzfM-2FvhSAncT-2Bv1ADsCytvSb323c-3D)
_RedState_
Erick Erickson

The sharp and rapid rise of Carly Fiorina is, for me at least, another indicator that polling right now is volatile and flawed. I actually think if primary season started tomorrow, we'd find the last men standing would be Sen. Ted Cruz (R-TX) 100% and Sen. Marco Rubio (R-FL) 92%, with Cruz having a slight advantage -- for now at least.

Polling has been largely wrong since 2010 and has been increasingly wrong after 2014. From showing many races in this country as too close to call to even missing the Tory blowout in Britain, something is wrong methodologically with polling. Likewise, the sharp spikes and declines, also too easy to predict, are too easily caught in flash polling. It does not make a lot of sense.

What does make sense and I think what remains are traditional campaign outlooks. I think Trump will fade, Fiorina will fade, and Carson is already fading. They are doing so because their momentum is based more on name identification and not on records or ground games.

If we look at traditional campaign data, which under the smoke and veneer of Campaign 2016 still matters, what we will find is that Sen. Ted Cruz (R-TX) 100% is laying down a hell of a ground game and has tons of cash with not nearly the burn rate that even Jeb Bush has. Cruz stands to profit the most from the collapse of Carson, Fiorina, and Trump -- all of whom are playing on the outsider advantages right now. Those advantages will start to go away as more traditional and necessary campaign tactics and strategies kick in like, for example, ballot access.

Cruz can get himself on ballots and get signatures collected. He can make a play through the SEC primary better than many of the other candidates can. He captures the conservative outsider angst while also being a more credible candidate long term than any of the other outsiders. Cruz has, after all, won an election and has a professional campaign team.

While conservatives will gravitate rapidly to Cruz, the more establishment oriented people who recognize the party still needs a fresh face and chage will likely go to Sen. Marco Rubio (R-FL) 92%. Already I'm hearing that both Walker and Bush donors are looking at Rubio as their next pick. Rubio has the highest positives of any of the candidates and is, in fact, the one Republican that the Democrats desperately fear because of his perceived ability to attract women, young voters, and Hispanics.

Likewise, Rubio has an experienced team that will be able to navigate ballot access laws, has consistently high polling in a volatile (and inaccurate) polling year, and did I mention just how positively people view him. Rubio also is running a tighter campaign with a slower burn rate than some of the others.

Being liked is a pretty big indicator of where people will head as their candidates start to fade. Rubio also has a pretty solid fundraising ability magnifying his nimble campaign's ability to get out there the closer we get to the primaries.

I think over the coming weeks, the campaign vultures will circle the Scott Walker campaign, looking to see if he has a pulse and, if they find none, will begin in earnest to pull voters and donors away from him. I suspect we'll see the more conservative elements head to Cruz and the more establishment elements head to Rubio.

Next month's debate performance could rupture it. There's still a lot that could change. But right now to me it looks like we are headed toward a Cruz vs. Rubio primary and, given how well the outsiders are doing currently, Cruz has a slight advantage.

---
---
| Paid for by Cruz for President
www.tedcruz.org
---
---

The rapid growth of our State Leadership Teams across America and the tremendous fundraising success we're seeing are making it increasingly clear to many that Ted Cruz is a front runner for the Republican nomination for President \-- So I just wanted to say thank you. Thank you for your support, thank you for making this all possible, and thank you in advance for everything that's yet to be done to ensure Ted is the Republican nominee for President! Mark Campbell National Political Director Cruz for President P.S. Tonight Ted will appear on the Kelly File on Fox News -- don't forget to tune in at 9 pm ET! RedState_ Erick Erickson The sharp and rapid rise of Carly Fiorina is, for me at least, another indicator that polling right now is volatile and flawed. I actually think if primary season started tomorrow, we'd find the last men standing would be Sen. Ted Cruz (R-TX) 100% and Sen. Marco Rubio (R-FL) 92%, with Cruz having a slight advantage -- for now at least. Polling has been largely wrong since 2010 and has been increasingly wrong after 2014. From showing many races in this country as too close to call to even missing the Tory blowout in Britain, something is wrong methodologically with polling. Likewise, the sharp spikes and declines, also too easy to predict, are too easily caught in flash polling. It does not make a lot of sense. What does make sense and I think what remains are traditional campaign outlooks. I think Trump will fade, Fiorina will fade, and Carson is already fading. They are doing so because their momentum is based more on name identification and not on records or ground games. If we look at traditional campaign data, which under the smoke and veneer of Campaign 2016 still matters, what we will find is that Sen. Ted Cruz (R-TX) 100% is laying down a hell of a ground game and has tons of cash with not nearly the burn rate that even Jeb Bush has. Cruz stands to profit the most from the collapse of Carson, Fiorina, and Trump -- all of whom are playing on the outsider advantages right now. Those advantages will start to go away as more traditional and necessary campaign tactics and strategies kick in like, for example, ballot access. Cruz can get himself on ballots and get signatures collected. He can make a play through the SEC primary better than many of the other candidates can. He captures the conservative outsider angst while also being a more credible candidate long term than any of the other outsiders. Cruz has, after all, won an election and has a professional campaign team. While conservatives will gravitate rapidly to Cruz, the more establishment oriented people who recognize the party still needs a fresh face and chage will likely go to Sen. Marco Rubio (R-FL) 92%. Already I'm hearing that both Walker and Bush donors are looking at Rubio as their next pick. Rubio has the highest positives of any of the candidates and is, in fact, the one Republican that the Democrats desperately fear because of his perceived ability to attract women, young voters, and Hispanics. Likewise, Rubio has an experienced team that will be able to navigate ballot access laws, has consistently high polling in a volatile (and inaccurate) polling year, and did I mention just how positively people view him. Rubio also is running a tighter campaign with a slower burn rate than some of the others. Being liked is a pretty big indicator of where people will head as their candidates start to fade. Rubio also has a pretty solid fundraising ability magnifying his nimble campaign's ability to get out there the closer we get to the primaries. I think over the coming weeks, the campaign vultures will circle the Scott Walker campaign, looking to see if he has a pulse and, if they find none, will begin in earnest to pull voters and donors away from him. I suspect we'll see the more conservative elements head to Cruz and the more establishment elements head to Rubio. Next month's debate performance could rupture it. There's still a lot that could change. But right now to me it looks like we are headed toward a Cruz vs. Rubio primary and, given how well the outsiders are doing currently, Cruz has a slight advantage. | Paid for by Cruz for President www.tedcruz.org | To unsubscribe please click | Mark Campbell P.O.
